creator: flz@xbsd.org
text:
On Mon, Jun 30, 2008 at 6:38 PM, Alex Kozlov <spam@rm-rf.kiev.ua> wrote:
> On Sat, Jun 21, 2008 at 01:58:30PM +0100, Florent Thoumie wrote:
> Sorry for delay. Stupid spamfilter.
>
>> I don't think this is a good idea as there's no drop-in replacement
>> for xorg-fonts. Liberation and webfonts are additional truetype fonts
>> but they can't replace xorg truetype fonts.
> Actualy it can. At least in some cases.
>
>> These macros were useful when multiple versions of the X distributions
>> were supported in the ports tree. Now there's only one and the only
>> reason why those macros aren't gone yet is because I haven't got to
>> removing them.
> If You are plan to remove these variables then this pr can be closed.
> Instead it need to modify x11-toolkits/pango to make X_FONTS_TTF_PORT
> dependency optional.

These macros will go eventually. In the meantime, I'd rather keep the
additional truetype fonts as additional, even if some of them as you
mentioned (I haven't found one) are drop-in replacement.

-- 
Florent Thoumie
flz@FreeBSD.org
FreeBSD Committer
